meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup: Accelerate Your Development Speed (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app is slow. Learn how to implement advanced analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to optimize your build process for success.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from lack of visibility. By integrating event-driven analytics into your CI/CD pipeline, you pinpoint bottlenecks instantly. Use Antigravity to instrument your codebase and leverage Claude Code to automate performance audits. This data-backed approach transforms your workflow from reactive debugging into proactive optimization, ensuring high-velocity feature shipping.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen, waiting for a build to finish, wondering why your velocity has stalled. The gap between your vision and your shipping speed is often hidden in the invisible friction of unmonitored workflows. Every second spent guessing where your code bottlenecks is a second lost in your journey to becoming a top-tier engineer.

The secret is not working harder; it is working with observability. By treating your development process like a production-grade SaaS product, you can strip away the inefficiencies that plague amateur builds. We are going to use the power of Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development environment into a high-performance engine.

steps.title: Instrumenting the Build Pipeline
steps.description: We start by injecting telemetry into your build scripts to measure execution time per module. This identifies exactly which files are slowing down your compilation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current build scripts and instrument them with high-resolution event logging to track execution time per module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'instrument build scripts for timing telemetry'

steps.title: Defining Performance Baselines
steps.description: Before you can optimize, you need a baseline. We will use Claude Code to run a series of benchmark tests against your current codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Establish a performance baseline for my current codebase, focusing on build latency and memory usage during hot-reloading.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'run baseline performance benchmarks on current project'

steps.title: Automating Bottleneck Detection
steps.description: Configure Antigravity to trigger alerts when build times exceed your defined threshold, preventing 'performance creep' in your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a monitoring hook in Antigravity that alerts when any build step exceeds 5 seconds of execution time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure automated alerts for build performance regressions'

steps.title: Refactoring Based on Telemetry
steps.description: Now, we use the collected data to refactor the slowest modules, using Claude Code to suggest algorithmic optimizations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Using the telemetry data, identify the most expensive function and refactor it for maximum efficiency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor high-latency modules based on telemetry data'

steps.title: Validating Throughput Improvements
steps.description: Finalize the process by verifying that the refactors actually improved your build speed without breaking existing functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a comparative analysis between the baseline and the current state to quantify total development speed improvement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'verify performance improvements and generate summary report'

pro_tips: Always log your build environment metadata (Node version, OS, RAM) alongside your timing data to identify hardware-specific bottlenecks.
Use sampling instead of tracking every single build to avoid adding overhead to your CI pipeline.
Store your telemetry logs in a time-series database to visualize your 'Velocity Trend' over the course of your project lifetime.
Treat your build scripts as production code; if it is worth building, it is worth unit-testing the build script itself.
Implement 'Cold-Start' vs 'Warm-Start' metrics separately, as they represent entirely different development pain points.
content.next_steps: Now that your analytics are live, integrate them into a dashboard using Grafana or a simple Notion table to track your weekly velocity improvements.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the tools to outpace your peers and build faster than ever before. Don't let slow development hold your genius back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: We start by injecting telemetry into your build scripts to measure execution time per module. This identifies exactly which files are slowing down your compilation.
Before you can optimize, you need a baseline. We will use Claude Code to run a series of benchmark tests against your current codebase.
Configure Antigravity to trigger alerts when build times exceed your defined threshold, preventing 'performance creep' in your project.
Now, we use the collected data to refactor the slowest modules, using Claude Code to suggest algorithmic optimizations.
Finalize the process by verifying that the refactors actually improved your build speed without breaking existing functionality.
